mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or held at very low levels, equity discount rates stay compressed and liquidity conditions support risk appetite, so S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days are positive.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive on average during falling-FEDFUNDS windows, or if the signal's rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL acceleration combined with a flat or rising FEDFUNDS rate tightens real rates and increases the odds of near-term equity multiple compression, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above its prior 6-month median
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not show negative average 20-day SPX returns, or if the joint inflation-rate condition fails to improve predictive power versus either proxy alone.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend signals weakening household income and demand, which should reduce corporate revenue expectations and drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.2 percentage points versus 3 months earlier
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-threshold episodes are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns more often than negative ones, or if the short-horizon effect vanishes in rolling out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized slowdown in RSAFS and HOUST indicates weakening final demand and housing spillovers, which should depress earnings expectations and push S&P 500 returns lower over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline versus their 3-month averages
failure_condition: Reject if joint declines in retail sales and housing starts do not precede negative 20-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term adds no predictive value beyond each series separately.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO is rising while PAYEMS is flat or falling, firms are extracting more output from fewer workers, which should support margins and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO is above its 3-month average while PAYEMS is below its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if productivity-up, payroll-flat/falling periods do not lead to positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the pattern repeatedly underperforms a no-signal benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: A broad increase in GDPC1 over the prior quarter signals stronger aggregate demand and revenue growth, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive quarterly GDPC1 growth does not correspond to above-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the relation disappears after revision-aware backtests.

mechanism_narrative: A low unemployment rate accompanied by strong payroll growth signals a resilient consumer base and lower recession risk, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE is at or below its 6-month average and PAYEMS is rising month over month
failure_condition: Reject if low-unemployment plus payroll-growth episodes do not outperform unconditional returns over 20 days, or if the signal fails when tested out of sample.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle, a rise in FEDFUNDS together with elevated CPIAUCSL keeps real policy restrictive, delaying multiple expansion and making S&P 500 returns negative over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises above its 6-month average while CPIAUCSL inflation remains above trend
failure_condition: Reject if tightening-plus-high-inflation regimes do not precede negative 60-day SPX returns, or if the effect is not statistically distinguishable from zero in rolling walk-forward tests.
